2019-02-25.abstract: For a decade now, the government has been slashing budgets for policing, housing and welfare.

2019-02-25.title: ‘Austerity, That’s What I Know’: The Making of a Young U.K. Socialist
2019-02-25.abstract: Alex McIntyre, 19, was brought up in a Britain being reshaped by government cuts. He gave up on capitalism after a year in college.

2019-02-25.title: Passenger Forces Bangladesh Plane to Land and Is Fatally Shot
2019-02-25.abstract: Special forces commandos stormed the plane after the passenger told soldiers that he wanted to speak with his wife and Prime Minister Sheikh Hasina.

2019-02-26.title: George Pell Sex Abuse Conviction Unsealed in Australia
2019-02-26.abstract: In December, Cardinal Pell was found guilty of sexually abusing two 13-year-old boys. But the verdict was ordered to be kept secret pending a second trial.

2019-02-26.title: Cardinal George Pell of Australia Convicted of Sexually Abusing Boys in 1996
2019-02-26.abstract: An Australian jury has convicted a powerful Vatican official of sexually abusing two boys during his time as Archbishop of Melbourne.

2019-02-26.title: U.N. Court Tells Britain to End Control of Chagos Islands, Home to U.S. Air Base
2019-02-26.abstract: The International Court of Justice said Britain acted unlawfully in 1965 when it detached a group of islands from Mauritius, at the time a British colony, to facilitate an American military lease.

2019-02-26.title: The Venezuela Battle Border
2019-02-26.abstract: Would soldiers turn away food for their own people? These images capture the day the Venezuelan opposition rolled the dice.

2019-02-26.title: Indian Jets Strike in Pakistan in Revenge for Kashmir Attack
2019-02-26.abstract: Pakistan’s military said aircraft from India had dropped bombs. But it was unclear what, if anything, was hit.

2019-02-27.title: Cold War-Style Accusations Fly as Security Council Meets on Venezuela
2019-02-27.abstract: The Trump administration’s special representative for Venezuela, Elliott Abrams, jousted with Russia’s ambassador, though nothing was resolved.

2019-02-27.title: Dozens Are Feared Buried in Indonesia Landslide
2019-02-27.abstract: At least two people were dead, and rescue workers were searching for survivors after a landslide struck an illegal gold mine on the island of Sulawesi.

2019-02-27.title: What a Korean War Peace Deal Could Mean, Decades After the Fighting Stopped
2019-02-27.abstract: The Trump-Kim meeting in Vietnam has raised expectations of an accord between North and South Korea that has been considered for decades.

2019-02-27.title: Trump-Kim Summit: Leaders Have Dinner in Vietnam
2019-02-27.abstract: President Trump and Kim Jong-un, the North Korean leader, dined on Wednesday before discussions start in earnest in Hanoi.

2019-02-27.title: Cardinal George Pell Is Jailed to Await Sentencing on Sexual Abuse Conviction
2019-02-27.abstract: Judge Peter Kidd of the County Court of Victoria said the cardinal, who was convicted in December of molesting two boys, would be sentenced on March 13.

2019-02-28.title: Trump-Kim Summit Updates: ‘Sometimes You Have to Walk,’ Trump Says as Talks Collapse
2019-02-28.abstract: President Trump and Kim Jong-un, the leader of North Korea, cut short their second and final day of talks in Hanoi, Vietnam.

2019-02-28.title: Trudeau’s Ex-Attorney General: ‘Veiled Threats’ Were Made to Drop Case
2019-02-28.abstract: Jody Wilson-Raybould said she was pressured to end a criminal prosecution of SNC-Lavalin. The opposition called for Mr. Trudeau’s resignation.

2019-02-28.title: A Sports Hijab Has France Debating the Muslim Veil, Again
2019-02-28.abstract: A French store dropped a plan to sell a hijab designed for runners after becoming embroiled in the country's debate about what Muslim women wear.

2019-02-28.title: Trump’s Talks With Kim Jong-un Collapse, and Both Sides Point Fingers
2019-02-28.abstract: President Trump said “sometimes you have to walk,” asserting that the talks with North Korea failed because the country was not prepared to do more to end its weapons programs.

2019-02-28.title: When It Comes to Negotiating, Kim Jong-un’s Team Is More Experienced
2019-02-28.abstract: As negotiators for the United States and North Korea sat down on Thursday in Hanoi, Vietnam, to discuss denuclearization, the team on Kim Jong-un’s side of the table had one clear advantage -- experience.
